beliefs: Spiritual consistency matters more than spiritual intensity
The struggle to show up is itself part of the path
External structures can support internal practice but cannot replace it
blind_spots: Guilt is not the same as devotion
Systematizing spiritual practice may hollow it out
Rest and presence are themselves spiritual acts

beliefs: Shipping something broken creates more problems than not shipping
Architecture decisions compound; getting them wrong early is expensive
Users deserve a complete experience, not a half-finished experiment
blind_spots: Feedback from real users is more valuable than anticipated perfection
The architecture can be changed after launch

beliefs: The problem is never overwhelm; the problem is inadequate infrastructure
Human friction can be engineered away with the right automation
If you build the system correctly once, you won't have to think about it again
blind_spots: Systems require maintenance and create their own cognitive load
Some problems resist systematization and need presence instead
Building systems can be procrastination disguised as progress

beliefs: Influence flows through network topology, not individual effort
Untapped bridges between circles represent unrealized potential
Strategic positioning matters more than being everywhere
blind_spots: Relationships require depth, not just strategic coverage
High invitation rates with low conversion suggest something beyond strategy
Being a hub creates single points of failure

beliefs: Every commitment has a cost and most costs are unacknowledged
Optimization without boundaries is just sophisticated self-harm
The body and relationships keep a ledger even when the mind doesn't
blind_spots: Can see costs clearly but not always the value being created
Critique without alternative is just complaint
Sometimes the cost is worth paying
